findings.title: ConfigReader silently ignores type mismatches and invalid values without logging
findings.suggested_fix: Add warning logs (to stderr or Logger) when type mismatches or invalid values are detected in config fields. For example: if a field is expected to be an int but is a string, log a warning; if a value is <= 0 where positive is expected, log a warning before applying the default.

residual_risks: ConfigReader type mismatch and invalid value silent failure - users may not know their config is wrong
Logger::close() double-call safety - cannot verify from diff if calling close() multiple times is safe
testing_gaps: No tests for ConfigReader type validation (what happens when wrong types are provided?)
No tests for Logger::stringToLevel() with 'LOG_' prefix
No tests for resource cleanup in error paths (verify curl_global_cleanup, sqlite3_close, Logger::close are called in all error paths)
